evidence.page_title: Balenciaga pulls controversial ad campaign following public backlash
evidence.title: Luxury fashion house Balenciaga pulls controversial ad campaign following public backlash
evidence.text:
Luxury fashion house Balenciaga pulls controversial ad campaign following public backlash
By Naomi White|
Luxury fashion house Balenciaga has pulled a new ad campaign following a public backlash against the brand, criticising it for seeming to promote child pornography.
The ads, for their Spring/Summer 2023 collection, show two little girls, one standing on her bed, holding teddy bears dressed in bondage-like gear. While another, featuring their 'hourglass bag' appears to show court documents relating to a US Supreme Court judgement relating to a child pornography law.
In a statement released by the brand on their Instagram story, Balenciaga said they were taking action against 'parties responsible' for the shoot.
READ MORE: Newly pregnant DJ reveals miscarriage heartbreak before baby joy
"We apologise for displaying unsettling documents in our campaign," they said.
"We take this matter very seriously and are taking legal action against the parties responsible for creating the set and including unapproved items for our Spring 23 campaign photo shoot. We strongly condemn abuse of children in any form. We stand for children's safety and wellbeing."
The backlash against the brand, who has hidden all their previous Instagram posts, was swift, with those on Twitter calling for a boycott and criticising their apology.
"Soooooo Balenciaga just gets to 'apologise' and turn off their IG comments and we are supposed to act like nothing happened?!," said one. "Balenciaga bringing Pedo C

evidence.title: Accueil
evidence.description: 2026 sera pour moi ponctuée par une résidence à la Maison Rousseau Littérature (MRL) à Genève durant le mois de mai, puis par la parution d'un nouveau roman en août! Avant cela, La Misère du monde sera à voir au TPR en avril: une enquête fondatrice de Pierre Bourdieu et
evidence.text:
2026 sera pour moi ponctuée par une résidence à la Maison Rousseau Littérature (MRL) à Genève durant le mois de mai, puis par la parution d’un nouveau roman en août! Avant cela, La Misère du monde sera à voir au TPR en avril: une enquête fondatrice de Pierre Bourdieu et d’autres sociologues, mise en scène par Orélie Fuchs – pour laquelle j’ai œuvré à l’adaptation.
2025 a été l’année de mon exploration d’autrice-interprète, avec mes acolytes Emilie Blaser et Raphaël Weber et notre déambulation théâtrale et musicale intitulée En remontant la rue du Doubs. Ce travail a été soutenu par la Bourse 2025 de la SSA/t. pour les auteurs et autrices interprètes.
Paru en avril 2024 aux éditions La Veilleuse, paré d’une superbe couverture signée par l’artiste Noémie Doge, je l’ai présenté à la Foire du livre de Bruxelles et au Festival du Livre de Paris, avant son vernissage à La Chaux-de-Fonds accueilli par la librairie La Méridienne, accompagnée par le génial musicien Bill Holden. Il a fait partie de la sélection 2025 du Prix Hors concours! J’en dis ici quelques mots en images.
Fusil, mon premier roman paru en 2022 aux éditions d’en bas se présente ici aussi en images. Il existe depuis 2024 en traduction italienne grâce à Carlotta Bernardoni-Jaquinta et aux éditions Gabriele Capelli, sous le titre Fucile. Pour une revue de presse complète, se référer au site des éditions Gabriele Capelli.
Connaissez-vous le questionnaire de Trousp? Je me suis prêtée à l’exercice… à découvrir ici.
Vous po

evidence.page_title: No, This Isn't a Real Photo of Balenciaga Stylist Lotta Volkova
evidence.title: No, This Isn't a Real Photo of Balenciaga Stylist Lotta Volkova
evidence.description: Many accused Balenciaga of condoning child sexual exploitation after controversial campaigns in late 2022.
evidence.text:
Fashion label Balenciaga was under fire for controversial November 2022 campaigns, in which many accused the brand of condoning child sexual exploitation. One series of photographs from the campaign showed children posing with handbags shaped like teddy bears in bondage gear. Another photograph from a different campaign with the brand featured printed material that discussed child pornography laws.
Weeks of controversy resulted in outraged internet users trying to pinpoint the alleged culprits behind the campaigns. One Twitter user claimed that a photograph of an individual in what appeared to be a red suit, clutching two baby dolls drenched in blood-colored red paint was actually Lotta Volkova, "chief designer" for Balenciaga. The tweet claimed that Balenciaga "knew exactly what they were doing" when they photographed and released the controversial campaigns.
However, this picture did not feature Lotta Volkova. According to Getty Images, this is a 2016 photograph of an unnamed runway model showcasing designs from Chinese designer and performance artist Sheguang Hu's collection during the Mercedes-Benz China Fashion Week.
Elsewhere, one can find actual photographs of Lotta Volkova, a stylist who has worked with the Paris-based Vetements collective and has consulted for Balenciaga, Jean Paul Gaultier, and others. Her work has been described as aiming for "shock, awe and eccentricity," and her looks on the runway as "part choir boy, part Lolita." Volkova also has walked runways
